blocks.body: A three-year science degree in a specialisation — Computer Science, Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Statistics or Life Sciences. It leads to industry, research or a master's (M.Sc, MCA, MBA), and is a strong base for data and analytics careers.

blocks.title: How you get in
blocks.body: 10+2 with science on merit, or a university entrance test (e.g. CUET).

blocks.title: At a glance
items: B.Sc Computer Science / Statistics is a common route into software and data roles.
Research-minded? A master's then a PhD is the usual path.
Many pair it with an MCA or MBA to broaden options.
